title: Bulbophyllum zophyranthum
intro: Bulbophyllum zophyranthum, also known as the Zephyr orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bulbophyllum zophyranthum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is made into various herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory issues, as well as being used in the form of a decoction or tincture for its medicinal properties.
taxonomy: Bulbophyllum zophyranthum is classified within the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Bulbophyllum, which is a large and diverse group of orchids. The species Bulbophyllum zophyranthum is further divided into various subspecies, each with distinct characteristics. The classification of Bulbophyllum zophyranthum is based on its unique morphological features and genetic makeup. The precise classification of Bulbophyllum zophyranthum may vary depending on the source and the latest taxonomic research.
